title: selaginella rothertii
intro: Selaginella rothertii, also known as the 'Cinnamon Fern', is a type of spore-producing, vascular plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing the respiratory system. Selaginella rothertii is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
